task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function meets all requirements and handles the edge case of an empty list correctly.
trace:
** 

I started by sorting the unique elements of the input list. Then, I iterated through the sorted list to identify consecutive sequences. If a sequence is found, it was added as a range (or single number) to the result list. Finally, I joined the result list into a string separated by commas. An edge case handled here is an empty input list, which returns an empty string.
